signals.title: KEF Spring Traffic Uptick - Week 12
signals.summary: ISAVIA weekly statistics indicate above-baseline international arrivals at Keflavik International Airport for the week commencing March 20. Primarily European leisure and stopover traffic, consistent with the seasonal spring recovery pattern.

signals.title: Yellow Weather Warning - Reykjanes Peninsula
signals.summary: Icelandic Met Office issued a yellow level warning for the southern coast and Reykjanes peninsula on March 21. Wind gusts up to 20 m/s forecast. No road closures indicated at time of issue. Moderate outdoor disruption risk.

signals.title: Cruise Call - Port of Reykjavik, March 22
signals.summary: Port of Reykjavik scheduled cruise call on March 22. Vessel capacity approximately 2,400 passengers. Status: scheduled. Estimated arrival window 07:00-09:00 local time. Passengers expected to disperse across Reykjavik and surrounding day-trip destinations.

signals.title: Reykjavik Arts Festival - Spring Preview Weekend
signals.summary: Reykjavik Arts Festival spring preview events scheduled across central Reykjavik venues on March 22 and 23. Multi-venue format attracting domestic and international cultural visitors. Official programme confirmed by the festival organisation.

signals.title: KEF Above-Baseline Arrivals - March 22
signals.summary: Weekend flight schedule shows above-baseline international inbound arrivals at KEF on March 22. Elevated widebody share noted. Pattern consistent with cruise-coincident demand and event-driven travel.

signals.title: Orange Weather Warning - Reykjanes Peninsula
signals.summary: Icelandic Met Office issued an orange level warning for the Reykjanes peninsula on March 24. Severe gales forecast with gusts up to 30 m/s. SafeTravel advisory issued simultaneously. Road access and outdoor visitor activity likely disrupted. Demand suppression expected.

signals.title: Visit Iceland Spring Campaign - Demand Uplift Window
signals.summary: Visit Iceland launched a spring promotional campaign targeting European short-break travellers for late March through mid-April. Organic demand uplift expected across spring weekends. Secondary confirmation from partner booking aggregators showing increased search interest.

signals.title: Reykjanes Cultural Heritage Weekend - Guided Geopark Tours
signals.summary: Reykjanes municipality scheduled a cultural heritage weekend with guided volcano and UNESCO Global Geopark tours on March 27. Promoted via official Visit Reykjanes tourism channels. Expected footfall in the 300-600 visitor range.

reasons: KEF arrivals above seasonal baseline for Week 12.
